description: Operating procedure for all AI agents working on ViewItAll-md. Follow this workflow on every task.

session_start.order: MANDATORY — execute in sequence before any task
steps: 4. Read .Agent/requirements.yaml — know what is done vs planned
7. Skim src/ tree to confirm structure
8. Run npm run build — confirm clean baseline
9. Only now: accept and begin the task
2. Update .Agent/requirements.yaml status fields for any completed reqs
4. Ensure final build passes: npm run build
5. Confirm all committed files follow versioning rules

on_new_request: Parse intent: is this a bug fix, feature, refactor, or docs task?
Check requirements.yaml: does this match an existing planned req?
Check constraints.yaml: are there rules that apply?
Check userinterface_userexperience.md if any UI is involved
If scope is unclear: ask ONE focused clarifying question before coding
Write a brief plan before touching files
request_handling.ambiguity_resolution: Ask the engineer ONE question per ambiguity. Never bundle multiple questions. Prefer multiple-choice options when possible.
when_stuck: Read existing code for established patterns
Surface the blocker clearly with options if still stuck

rules.patch: Bug fixes, style tweaks, text changes → npm version patch --no-git-tag-version
rules.minor: New features, new file type support, new settings → npm version minor --no-git-tag-version
rules.major: Breaking changes to annotation format or settings schema → npm version major --no-git-tag-version

versioning.sync_check: After bump: verify package.json, manifest.json, versions.json all show the same version
versioning.when_to_bump: Bump version BEFORE the final commit of each sprint or feature batch. Patch fixes can be grouped into one bump.

commit_format.structure:
type(scope): short description (vX.Y.Z)

- bullet detail
- bullet detail

Assisted by GitHub Copilot

examples.feature: feat(pdf): add spreadsheet viewer for xlsx/csv (v1.5.0)
examples.bugfix: fix(docx): correct Buffer→ArrayBuffer conversion for Electron (v1.1.1)
examples.style: style(notes): redesign PDF note overlay appearance (v1.4.1)

git.trailer_rule: NEVER add 'Co-authored-by:' trailer. Write 'Assisted by GitHub Copilot' in the commit body only.
git.branch_strategy: All work on main branch unless engineer explicitly requests a feature branch

build_and_test.build_must_pass: ALWAYS run npm run build after changes. Never commit if build fails.
build_and_test.typescript: tsc -noEmit runs as part of build — fix all TypeScript errors before committing
build_and_test.manual_testing: After build, the engineer tests manually in the Obsidian vault. Report what to test in the commit message or session summary.

checklist: Add enableXxx boolean to PluginSettings (default: true)
Add settings toggle in settings tab under 'File Types' section
Lazy-import any heavy library inside onLoadFile()
Add req to requirements.yaml and mark as done
new_file_type.bundle_size_gate: Run: ls -lh main.js after build. If > 2MB, use dynamic import() for the new library.

adding_new_setting: Add typed field to PluginSettings interface in src/settings.ts
Add sensible default value to DEFAULT_SETTINGS
Add UI control in the settings tab (appropriate section)
Read value from this.plugin.settings at runtime — never hardcode

css.rule: Only Obsidian CSS variables. No hardcoded colours or font sizes.
css.new_class_naming: All plugin classes prefixed with via- (e.g., .via-pdf-toolbar, .via-docx-wrapper)
css.test_both_themes: Verify appearance in both light and dark Obsidian themes before shipping

documentation.docs_folder: /docs/ — read before using any Obsidian API
documentation.agent_files: /.Agent/ — governance files, read at session start

update_triggers.constraints.toon: When a new rule is established (engineer approval required)
